name: Heroes Feast
description: A lavish spread that temporarily boosts all attributes. Requires preparation time.

name: Iron Caltrops
description: Spiked balls that slow pursuing enemies.

name: Bear Trap
description: Heavy trap that immobilizes large creatures.

name: Teleport Crystal
description: Shatters to return the party to safety instantly.

name: Tome of Knowledge
description: Ancient text that grants permanent skill knowledge.

name: Charm of Fortune
description: Increases item drop rates for the next encounter.

name: Tiny Fangs
description: Tiny creature fangs. A small, clean bite.

name: Small Fangs
description: Small venomous fangs. A chance to poison on each bite.

name: Goblin Dagger
description: A crude iron blade.

name: Goblin Shortbow
description: A crude bow.

name: Goblin Cleaver
description: A crude goblin cleaver. Heavy and unbalanced but effective.

name: Minor Health Potion
description: A pale green vial that closes minor wounds. Standard kit for any adventurer.

name: Health Potion
description: A crimson vial that mends significant injuries. Familiar in every healer's satchel.

name: Minor Mana Potion
description: A faintly glowing blue vial that restores a small amount of mana.

name: Mana Potion
description: A deep violet vial that restores a meaningful reserve of mana.

name: Minor Stamina Potion
description: An amber vial that eases muscle fatigue and restores minor stamina.

name: Escape Rope
description: Guarantees a successful retreat from combat.

name: Smoke Bomb
description: A ceramic sphere packed with choking smoke. Guarantees a clean retreat.

name: Throwing Knife
description: A quick ranged attack dealing moderate damage.

name: Large Fangs
description: Large venomous fangs. Deals poison damage on every hit, with a chance to inflict strong poison.

name: Great Fangs
description: Massive venomous fangs. Lethal venom on every strike.

name: Shaman Totem
description: A crude shamanic focus carved from bone.

name: Strange Mushrooms
description: Pungent fungi favoured by Orc berserkers. Effects are unpredictable but undeniably powerful.

name: Halfling Silver Coin
description: Old currency, stamped with a river-knot on one face and a halfling hearth on the other. Someone kept this for a reason.

name: Vulture Company Ledger
description: A detailed record of every caravan the Vulture Company has raided, who they paid for advance information, and what the garrison at this tower actually found before they disappeared. Several pages are water-damaged. The ones that aren't are worse.

name: Moon-Touched Apple
description: The blight has not entirely destroyed what the Order made. These still glow faintly at night. The ones grown before Sister Maren's ritual taste of cold water and old prayers. They are used as offerings at Verdant Word sites where the old faith still holds.

name: Ironvein Loyalist Seal
description: An official seal of the Ironvein Loyalists, authorizing the bearer to move supplies through Loyalist-held territory. To the Goldcrown Usurpers, it is evidence. To the Loyalists, it is a passport. To anyone at the Broken Axle Crossing, it is a liability.

name: Royal Guard Insignia
description: Melted at the edges but still readable. The garrison number is scorched off but the crest is intact. In some parts of the Vale, presenting this to the right person opens doors. In others, it marks you as someone who has been somewhere they should not have been.

name: Verdant Word Seal
description: A seal used by the Order of the Verdant Word to mark sites under their protection. Still functional — the ward magic bound into it recognizes other Order sites and responds to them. At locations where the Order once worked, presenting this changes how the remaining spirits behave.

name: Merchant Guild Seal
description: The Merchant Guild's official seal of authorization. In the Capital it opens certain doors. At the Bastion of the Fractured Ward, the Guild-funded guards still recognize it. Guildmaster Vane's personal seal carries additional weight — evidence of his family's involvement in the Verdant Word coup that someone with the right connections might find very useful.

name: Oath Stone
description: A pact-rune stone from the Shrine of the First Oath, still holding a fragment of the original binding frequency between the halfling elders and Lyra. At sites where the pact is actively breaking — the Marsh of Broken Oaths, the Salt-Wept Beacon — presenting this temporarily stabilizes the ward. It does not fix anything. It buys time.

name: Ironvein Ancestral Signet
description: A signet ring bearing the mark of the Ironvein Forge-Kings — not the current Loyalist faction, but the original line that the ancestors served. At the Hall of Ancestral Echoes, the spirits recognize it. It does not grant safe passage. It grants acknowledgment, which is different and more useful.

name: Monitoring Crystal
description: A calibrated crystal from the joint dwarven watchtower above the Sunken Sanctum. It was designed to track the water spirit's resonance frequency. At the Sanctum itself, it still works — revealing patterns in Archbishop Malacor's movements that a party without it cannot see.

name: Athenaeum Sealed Scroll
description: A scroll from the Whispering Athenaeum's restricted archive. The wax seal bears the mark of the High Council of the Verdant Word — the same council whose disappearance the Capital sealed the Spire of Silent Conviction to contain. The scroll is addressed to someone by name. The name has been redacted.

name: Ancestral Verdict
description: A sealed record from the Hall of Ancestral Echoes bearing the High Judge's mark — the formal outcome of Thorgrim's assessment of the succession claimants. Both factions know this document exists. Neither faction wants it read publicly. In the right hands at the right location, it is the most dangerous piece of paper in Khaz-Morad.

name: The Unpaid Ledger
description: The Stone-Tusk Clan's record of services rendered to the Goldcrown Usurpers and payment not received. Every raid, every deployment, every casualty, itemized and dated. The final entry is a running total that has been updated weekly for five years. In the Guildhall of Silent Trade, this document is more damaging than anything the Verdant Auditors have managed to produce. The Merchant Guild helped structure the original contract. They knew it would not be honored.

name: The Unpaid Ledger
description: The Stone-Tusk Clan's copy of the original Goldcrown contract, annotated by Krog across five years with every payment missed, every deadline passed, and every messenger who arrived with excuses instead of coin. The annotations are precise and damning. At the Guildhall of Silent Trade, this document is evidence of Merchant Guild collusion in the Usurpers' decision to default. Guildmaster Vane's name appears in the margin of the final page.

name: Seed of the First Tree
description: A seed from the First Tree of the Silverbough, given by the Preservation Core or taken from the Fragment's Reckoning. It carries the oldest nature magic in the Vale — pre-Verdant Word, pre-pact, pre-Fraying. At the Sunken Sanctum of Aethelgard, it can purify a portion of the water corruption permanently, reducing the Archbishop's power in the final encounter. The elves made this possible fifty years ago by choosing not to use it.

name: Lyra's Current
description: Not a weapon — the force of a river spirit's attention made physical. Contact with it carries the cold weight of deep water.

name: Tidebound Harpoon
description: A harpoon forged from salvaged ship metal, treated with the water spirit's blessing until it conducts lightning through salt water. The Tidebound use it to pin and shock simultaneously.

name: Fraying Touch
description: The hands of the Fraying-Touched carry residue that transfers on contact. They cannot help it. Some of them have stopped touching things they care about.

name: Ashen Fist
description: Their armor fused to their skin in the explosion. Their fists conduct the residual fire magic permanently. Every hit transfers a trace of it.

name: Corrupted Bite
description: The Alpha's jaw has been reshaping for five years under exposure to the Whispering Woods' residual magic. What its bite transfers now is not venom — it is a low-grade nature confusion that the woods have been perfecting for centuries.

name: Ancestral Verdict
description: The hammer Thorgrim carried when he presided over the first coronation, four hundred years ago. A blow from it carries the weight of four centuries of accumulated judgment. Recipients report a brief, total cessation of will.

name: Purifying Touch
description: Elara's hands carry fifty years of the First Tree's magic. Contact with them applies the Gallery's purification protocol directly — stripping mana access the way a surgeon strips infected tissue. She does not experience this as violence.

name: Snapping Jaws
description: The bite of a hunting hound — not venomous, but the tearing grip draws blood.

name: Powerful Jaws
description: The crushing bite of a large predator. Tears flesh and breaks rhythm.

name: Sharp Claws
description: Fast raking claws. Leave shallow cuts that accumulate.

name: Raking Claws
description: Heavy claws that tear and puncture in the same stroke.

name: Iron Greatsword
description: A heavy iron blade requiring both hands to swing effectively.

name: Oak Claymore
description: A reinforced wooden blade used for training and basic defense.

name: Steel Greatsword
description: A well-forged steel blade balanced for heavy combat.

name: Bronze Claymore
description: A durable bronze weapon favored for its resilience.

name: Mithril Greatsword
description: A surprisingly light greatsword made of rare mithril.

name: Frost Greatblade
description: A blade that freezes the air around it.

name: Adamant Greatsword
description: An adamant blade impossibly sharp and strong.

name: Flame Greatblade
description: An greatsword enchanted to burn enemies on contact.

name: Celestial Greatsword
description: A massive sword radiating divine energy.

name: Void Greatblade
description: A blade that cuts through the fabric of reality.

name: Wind Greatblade
description: Enchanted to slice through air with zero resistance.

name: Blood Greatsword
description: A cursed blade that thirsts for vital essence.

name: Dragon Greatsword
description: Forged from the scales and teeth of an ancient wyrm.

name: Phoenix Greatsword
description: Rebirth awaits those who walk in fire with this blade.

name: Ascension Greatblade
description: Walk among the gods with this divine instrument.

name: Woodcutter's Greataxe
description: A repurposed forestry tool with a wedge-shaped head, heavy enough to split logs or limbs.

name: Iron Bardiche
description: A long blade mounted on a pole, favored by mercenaries for its reaching cleave.

name: Bronze Ono
description: A ceremonial bronze axe with a wide bit, used in rituals and frontier defense.

name: Steel Executioner
description: A broad-headed axe designed for a single, decisive downward strike.

name: Mithril Splitter
description: A lightweight axe that retains momentum, allowing for faster recovery between swings.

name: Storm Cleaver
description: Forged during a thunderstorm; the edge crackles with residual lightning.

name: Adamant Reaver
description: An unbreakable edge that bites deep into any material.

name: Venom Chopper
description: Grooves along the blade channel toxin directly into the wound.

name: Frostbite Bardiche
description: Cold radiates from the edge, slowing blood and movement.

name: Tectonic Splitter
description: A massive axe that sends foes reeling with every impact.

name: Radiant Executioner
description: Blessed steel that burns the wicked on contact.

name: Berserker's Ono
description: Feeds on combat, increasing aggression with every successful cleave.

name: Void Reaver
description: Cuts through magical wards as easily as flesh.

name: Nightfall Greataxe
description: Drains the light from the air with every swing.

name: Inferno Splitter
description: A blade forged in the heart of a volcano; everything it touches ignites.

name: Recruit's Spear
description: A simple iron tip on ash wood, drilled for formation thrusts.

name: Ashwood Halberd
description: A rustic polearm with a heavy iron head, built for chopping and hooking.

name: Steel Glaive
description: A balanced polearm with a curved blade, ideal for sweeping attacks.

name: Bronze Billhook
description: A versatile polearm with a hook for tripping foes and pulling shields.

name: Mithril Pike
description: A lightweight mithril spear that never loses its edge.

name: Zephyr Halberd
description: A wind-enchanted polearm that disrupts enemy footing on impact.

name: Adamant Glaive
description: An impossibly hard blade mounted on a reinforced haft.

name: Shadowreach Naginata
description: A curved blade that drains harmony with each strike.

name: Sentinel's Glaive
description: A ceremonial polearm blessed to protect the realm through tactical control.

name: Stormcaller Pike
description: A lightning-charged spear that arcs electricity through enemy ranks.

name: Stormwind Pike
description: A polearm that cuts through air resistance, allowing rapid repositioning.

name: Bloodthorn Halberd
description: A thorned polearm that delivers venom with every thrust.

name: Dragonspine Glaive
description: A polearm forged from the spine of a fallen dragon, burning with residual fury.

name: Voidrend Naginata
description: A curved blade that tears at the fabric of magic with each sweep.

name: World-Spine Naginata
description: Carved from the first tree's branch; each thrust echoes with cosmic disorientation.

name: Iron Earthbreaker
description: A crude iron head studded with blunt spikes, designed to dent plate and shatter bone.

name: Oak Great Club
description: A reinforced hardwood club, iron-banded and heavy enough to break shields.

name: Bronze Lucerne
description: A pronged hammer face for crushing, with a reinforced spike for piercing gaps in armor.

name: Steel Longhammer
description: Carved with monstrous faces and drilled holes that whistle before a concussive strike.

name: Mithril Maul
description: Light enough to swing, dense enough to shatter steel on impact.

name: Wolf-Tooth Planoson
description: A tapered pole bound in metal with a narrow spike, usable as a club or top-heavy spear.

name: Adamant Earthbreaker
description: An unbreakable head that never dulls, capable of flattening plate armor.

name: Graveweight Club
description: Forged from unnaturally dense metal; each swing saps the strength from foes.

name: Silence Lucerne
description: A heavy head that induces concussions severe enough to disrupt speech and casting.

name: Tectonic Maul
description: A massive hammer that sends shockwaves through the ground on impact.

name: Exhaustion Longhammer
description: Each blow drains the stamina from foes, leaving them too tired to fight.

name: Iron Planoson
description: A refined pole-hammer balancing crush and pierce for armored duels.

name: Cursed Earthbreaker
description: Forged in a lightless vault; strikes drain vitality and cloud the mind.

name: Dreambreaker Club
description: A knockout weapon that renders foes unconscious with a single solid hit.

name: Omega Lucerne
description: The final word in blunt force; each impact shatters armor and will alike.

name: Ash Shortbow
description: A simple wooden bow strung with hemp, reliable for hunting small game.

name: Bone Longbow
description: Crafted from layered bone and sinew, offering greater draw weight.

name: Iron Warbow
description: Reinforced with iron laths to maintain tension in damp conditions.

name: Composite Bow
description: Layers of wood, horn, and sinew create a snappy, powerful release.

name: Viper Bow
description: Arrows dipped in neurotoxin; a grazing hit is often fatal.

name: Dustwind Bow
description: Fires specialized arrows that burst into blinding sand on impact.

name: Frostbite Bow
description: Enchanted to chill the air; targets are highlighted by frost trails.

name: Thunderstring
description: The string hums with static; shots disrupt enemy focus.

name: Ember Bow
description: Arrows ignite mid-flight, creating flashes that disorient targets.

name: Umbral Bow
description: Shadows cling to the arrows, weighing down the enemy's movement.

name: Dreamer's Bow
description: Fires tranquilizer darts; targets slump into unconsciousness.

name: Hush Bow
description: Makes no sound when drawn; shots disrupt vocal components of magic.

name: Glacier Bow
description: Forged from eternal ice; arrows lock joints solid on impact.

name: Mirage Bow
description: Shots bend light; targets become disoriented and attack allies.

name: Voidstring
description: Drawn from the void; shots sever the connection to magical energy.

name: Apprentice Staff
description: A simple wooden staff carved with beginner runes, channeling faint arcane energy.

name: Bone Rod
description: Crafted from layered bone and sinew, offering greater mana capacity.

name: Iron Scepter
description: Reinforced with iron bands to maintain focus in damp conditions.

name: Crystal Wand
description: Layers of crystal and wood create a resonant channel for magic.

name: Frost Staff
description: Infused with glacial essence; spells crystallize the air.

name: Ember Staff
description: A core of eternal flame amplifies spell potency.

name: Void Scepter
description: Draws from the void; spells drain enemy mana reserves.

name: Harmony Rod
description: Attuned to natural rhythms; healing and support spells resonate.

name: Shadow Weaver
description: Weaves shadows into spells; disrupts enemy healing and support.

name: Barrier Staff
description: Creates magical shields; reduces incoming damage for the wielder.

name: Life Leech Staff
description: Drains vitality from foes to sustain the caster.

name: Protection Scepter
description: Blessed protection reduces all incoming damage for allies.

name: Cosmic Staff
description: Infused with starlight; all attributes are enhanced.

name: Divine Staff
description: Channel divine power; healing and support spells are greatly enhanced.

name: Genesis Staff
description: A staff that holds the power to create worlds; all magic is amplified.

name: Stamina Potion
description: An amber vial that drives back exhaustion and restores meaningful stamina.

name: Major Health Potion
description: A deep red vial pulsing with restorative energy. Closes even grievous wounds.

name: Major Mana Potion
description: A vial of condensed arcane essence. Fully replenishes most casters's reserves.

name: Major Stamina Potion
description: A potent amber draught that banishes fatigue entirely and restores full stamina reserves.

name: Antidote
description: A bitter tincture that purges poison, disease, and minor curses from the body.

name: Elixir of Vitality
description: A luminous golden draught that fully restores health and cleanses all debuffs. Alchemists guard the recipe jealously.

name: Hearty Meal
description: A proper cooked meal — bread, broth, and salted meat. Restores stamina and a measure of focus.

name: The Ferryman's Pole
description: The crew of the Moonlit Ferry never left. Neither did their tools. The pole still knows the current.

name: The Mage's Hearth
description: A garrison mage tried to bind a fire spirit to warm the outpost. The garrison did not survive. The staff kept the binding.

name: Ink of the Bound Scribes
description: The Athenaeum's scribes died at their desks fifty years ago. The blood-ink preserved them and their work both. They have not stopped writing.

name: Verdict of the Ancestors
description: The Forge-Kings' coronation blade. The ancestors have been conducting a trial for four years. Neither delegation returned with a favorable verdict. The sword did not come with them.

name: Spear of the Sunken City
description: Aethelgard sank in a single night. The survivors made a religion of it. This spear was used in the ritual that bound the water spirit. The binding held. The city did not.

name: Thrain's Field Orders
description: Sealed route schedules countersigned by Watch Commander Thrain, formatted as reporting intervals for an unnamed recipient. The sigil pressed into each page is not dwarven.

name: Blood-Oath Token
description: A contract token bearing Vane's seal and the Stonetusk mark — proof that the Vulture Company's outstanding debt to the Orcs was acknowledged, not just ignored.

name: Third Heir's Signet
description: A dwarven seal ring belonging to the Third Heir of Khaz-Morad — proof of lineage that neither Ironvein nor Goldcrown can legally dispute.

name: Thrain's Complete Report
description: Watch Commander Thrain's unredacted field report — the full version, not the summary sent to the Capital. It names the Spire as the flood's destination and identifies the directing sigil. Someone intercepted the summary before it reached the High Council.

name: Styptic Powder
description: Camp-made coagulant scraped from boiled bark — it won't heal you, but it will stop the bleeding and the worst of the weakness long enough to matter.

name: Stamina Ration
description: Dried meat and pressed grain bound in wax cloth. The stimulants packed inside are rough but they cut through exhaustion fast enough to matter.

name: Hrolf's Standing Orders
description: A sealed dispatch bearing the Royal Crest, dated five years ago. It authorizes Captain Hrolf to maintain the Capital perimeter at his discretion. It does not contain an end condition.

name: The Petitioners' Verdict
description: A rubbing taken from the Throne's verdict stone beneath the dais. Both claimant sigils are struck through. A third sigil is partially legible — enough to establish lineage, not enough to name the heir without the Ancestral Forge records.

name: Sigil Fragment
description: A shard of the original Verdant Word binding glyph, inverted. The inversion is not damage — it is the original design flaw, preserved. Intended for use at the Spire of Fractured Time.

name: Coronation Writ
description: The Forge-Kings' official record of the Third Heir's succession claim — complete lineage, unbroken from the original founding. Both factional claims are annotated in the margin as derivative. The annotation is four hundred years old.

name: Unified Seed
description: A seed from the First Tree grown after the reconciliation — carrying both the Core's silence and the Fragment's fifty-year pact. Intended for use at the Spire of Fractured Time.

name: The Architect's Seal
description: The Sigil-Bearer's coordination sigil — proof that the Architect operates through human vessels by choice, not necessity. Intended for use at the Spire of Fractured Time.

name: First Hand Shard
description: A fragment of the First Hand's dissolution — crystallized Architect-attention, still warm. Proof that the Architect can localize itself deliberately. Intended for use at the Spire of Fractured Time.

name: Resonance Node Fragment
description: A piece of the Architect's anchor node — the point where it tethers itself to a location. The Architect did not know this could be removed. It knows now. Intended for use at the Spire of Fractured Time.

name: Ferryfolk Lantern
description: A halfling ferryman's brass lantern, still lit. The flame does not flicker even underwater. It was found floating above the station's collapsed lower deck the morning after Bram died.

name: Heir's Signet
description: A dwarven signet ring carrying the Third Heir's seal — not a decoration but a legal instrument. Its presence at a contract ceremony confers ancestral recognition. The Goldcrown faction did not know a Third Heir existed until this ring appeared in the Guildhall records.

name: Songbook of Lyra
description: A halfling songbook containing the complete True Name of Lyra in its original melodic notation — not as words, but as intervals. The High Cantor spent forty years memorizing it before committing it to paper, on the theory that a song written down is a song that cannot be silenced by killing the singer.

name: The Capital Key
description: The master key to the Capital's inner gate, taken from the Gatehouse the morning Hrolf's guard died holding it open. It still turns. The lock it fits no longer exists, but the key carries the authorization frequency of every Royal Guard officer who ever held command of the Capital's defenses. All of them. Including the ones who gave the wrong orders.

name: The Dissolution Record
description: The original vote record from the Verdant Word's final session — seven folios, three dissenting signatures still active and audible as a faint harmonic when held. It does not prove the Architect exists. It proves the Architect was invited. That distinction is the only weapon that works at the Spire's core.

name: The Archbishop's Drowned Scepter
description: Malacor carried this into the Sanctum when it sank and carried it back out fifty years later. The wood has petrified around the cold channel. It still broadcasts the Sanctum's frequency to anyone with enough harmony to hear it.

name: Grik's Clan Axe
description: Every Grizzlethorn chief carries an axe notched once for each enemy killed in single combat. Grik's has forty-three notches. The forty-fourth was going to be for the player. It was not.

name: Justicar's Verdict Plate
description: Thorgrim wore this plate to every session of the Ancestral Court for sixty years. The runes etched into the breastplate are not decorative — they are the verbatim text of the three most contested verdicts he ever delivered. He was right on all three. He was not always kind.

name: Bone Circlet
description: Grizzlethorn goblins string these from the bones of small animals and the dried sinew of whatever they last ate. Crude by any standard except the one that measures whether it stops a blade.

name: Woven Bark Mask
description: The Willow shrine spirits weave these from living bark and bind them with vine. They hold the shape of whatever the wearer was feeling when the shrine last acknowledged them.

name: Iron Coif
description: Standard issue for the Highland Watch before the succession crisis cut the supply lines. Most of what the Frostbite Conscripts are wearing is still functional — the cold preserved it.

name: Barnacle-Crusted Helm
description: The Tidebound don't remove the barnacle growth from their gear. After a certain point the growth becomes structural — the barnacles are holding the helm together as much as the original ironwork.

name: Bone-Plate War Helm
description: The Bone-Guards plate their helms with the skulls of enemies from previous contracts. The bone is labeled the same way their armor pieces are — campaign name, opponent, outcome. The Stone-Tusk keep records.

name: Canopy Hood
description: The Gallery elves weave these from the upper canopy where the light still reaches. The stitching incorporates a faint warding sigil that the weavers do not explain and visitors do not ask about.

name: Capital Guard Coif
description: Royal Guard issue, five years worn. The Capital crest on the brow-plate has been scratched off by someone who did not want to be identified as Guard while doing what they were doing at the Barricades.

name: Tidebound Warden Helm
description: Forged from salvaged ship hull plating and sealed with the Sanctum's waterproofing ritual. The eye-slits are designed to work at depth. The Tide Wardens wear these into flooded chambers without adjusting their stride.

name: Reinforced Leather Coat
description: Gallery elves reinforce their field coats with hardened bark-plate at the shoulders and ribs — mobile enough for canopy work, solid enough that the pruners don't hesitate before they swing.

name: Tide-Hardened Hauberk
description: Tidebound chain treated with mineral salt from the Sanctum's deep water to prevent rust. The rings have closed tighter over the years. It fits differently than it did when it was issued.

name: Shadow-Stitched Leather
description: The Gallery's silence weavers thread shadow-thread into the seams of their coats — not for protection but to muffle the sound of movement. The effect is functional. The stitching is also genuinely beautiful, which the weavers consider irrelevant.

name: Warden's Chain
description: Royal Guard issue for officers who held gate or checkpoint duty. The reinforced shoulders carry extra plate rings where a sword would naturally fall. It is not comfortable. Nobody who wore it for five years at the Barricades complained about comfort.

name: Spire Fragment
description: A fragment of the Spire of Fractured Time, recovered after the Architect was contained. It hums at a frequency that changes depending on how many times the bearer has stood at the Spire's door. Each run leaves a different mark. The fragment records all of them.

name: Spire Fragment
description: A fragment of the Spire of Fractured Time, recovered after the Architect was contained. It hums at a frequency that changes depending on how many times the bearer has stood at the Spire's door. Each run leaves a different mark. The fragment records all of them.
